s1.title: Optimizing Hash Map for Two Sum
s1.explanation: Using a Hash Map to store visited values helps us search for the 'complement' (target - current_val) in O(1) constant time.

s2.title: Most Intuitive One-pass Approach
s2.explanation: Similar to Python, Map in JS is extremely powerful for this problem.

s3.title: Using Array instead of Hash Map (Optimization)
s3.explanation: If the array is already sorted, we can use the Two Pointers algorithm to reduce memory space to O(1).

terminal.placeholder: Press 'Run Code' to see compilation results.

p1.title: Two Sum
p1.description:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

You can return the answer in any order.

tutor.disclaimer: AI Tutor can make mistakes. Please check important information.

mock.welcome: Hello! I am LearnWell's AI Tutor. I see you are working on '{{problem}}'. Do you need a hint or explanation for any part?
mock.suggestion: An optimal approach for this is using a Hash Map to store visited values. Would you like me to explain how to use a Hash Map for this problem?

badges.notUsed: Learning without hints will earn you the "Self-Reliant" Badge 🎖️

review.title: AI Code Review Result
review.description: Your code follows the correct logic. However, it can be further optimized for memory.
review.tip: Instead of using nested O(n²) loops, try using a Hash Map to achieve O(n).

mock.h1: Have you considered the case where the input array is empty or has only 1 element? Think about the loop termination condition.
mock.h2: You can use a Hash Map to store the complement (target - current_value). This helps find the remaining element in O(1).
